steps.title: Interpret the percentage increases for each species
steps.content:
Let the starting count of each species be $x$. When a quantity increases by 1,900%, the new value equals the original plus 1,900% of the original:
$$\text{Prey end} = x + 1900\% \cdot x = x + 19x = 20x$$
Similarly, a 150% increase gives:
$$\text{Predators end} = x + 150\% \cdot x = x + 1.5x = 2.5x$$

steps.title: Use the percentage comparison formula
steps.content:
When one quantity is $p\%$ greater than another, their ratio is:
$$\frac{\text{Larger}}{\text{Smaller}} = 1 + \frac{p}{100}$$
Here, prey end is $p\%$ greater than predators end:
$$\frac{20x}{2.5x} = 1 + \frac{p}{100}$$
Cancel $x$ and simplify:
$$\frac{20}{2.5} = 8 = 1 + \frac{p}{100}$$

steps.title: Solve for p
steps.content:
Subtract 1 from both sides:
$$8 - 1 = \frac{p}{100}$$
$$7 = \frac{p}{100}$$
Multiply both sides by 100:
$$p = 700$$
